Title: Enhanced Production of Rebaudioside D and Rebaudioside M through V155T Substitution in the Glycosyltransferase UGT91D2 from Stevia rebaudiana
Journal: Journal of Agricultural and Food Chemistry

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Minor steviol glycosides such as dulcoside A and rebaudioside J
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase; close homolog of UGT91D2
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Shows weak side activity leading to small increases in Dul A and Reb J, but does not support major SG glucosylation like UGT91D2
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in N. benthamiana with substrate feeding and LC-MS/MS; proteomics-based protein quantification
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT91D1, a 95% identical homolog of UGT91D2, was used as a comparator in transient assays. 2. It showed small but significant increases in minor rhamnosylated products Dul A and Reb J. 3. It lacked the major productive SG glucosylation activities seen with UGT91D2.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited value for boosting major high-value Reb D/Reb M production based on this study; useful as a comparator for understanding donor/substrate specificity
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Weak activity may divert flux to minor side products rather than desirable major sweeteners
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves with feeding of S13G, Rub, Stv, or Reb A
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reported previously as having no SG activity, but here weak activity toward minor side products was detected; protein accumulation was slightly lower than UGT91D2 in transient assays
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics (DIA LC-MS/MS), ProteomeXchange: PXD056227; jPOST: JPST003380

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late steviol glycoside glycosylation; β-1,2 glycosylation on both R1 and R2 sides
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose mainly from UDP-glucose to steviol glycosides through β-1,2 glucosylation on the R1 and R2 sugar chains, converting substrates such as S13G to Sb, Rub to Stv, Stv to Reb E, and Reb A to Reb D; also shows side reactions using non-glucose donors to form minor SGs such as Dul A and Reb J
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: S13G; Rub; Stv; Reb A; UDP-Glc
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Sb; Stv; Reb E; Reb D
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target products and immediate precursors for highly glucosylated SGs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Noncaloric natural sweeteners with superior sweetness and organoleptic properties
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves; heterologously expressed in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase; PSPG box-containing plant secondary product glycosyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a bottleneck, especially for R2-side glycosylation leading to Reb D and Reb M
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of highly glucosylated steviol glycosides, but native enzyme has limited R2-side activity, restricting Reb D and Reb M accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Structure-guided mutagenesis; heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae with metabolite profiling; transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana with substrate feeding and LC-MS/MS; proteomics-based protein quantification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT91D2 is the key late glycosyltransferase acting as a bottleneck for formation of highly glucosylated steviol glycosides. 2. It catalyzes β-1,2 glucosylation on both R1 and R2 sides, but R2-side activity is much weaker than R1-side activity. 3. Native UGT91D2 supports production of Reb D and Reb M but also forms side products through ambiguous UDP-sugar usage.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important engineering target for increasing high-value sweeteners Reb D and Reb M in stevia or heterologous platforms; can be stacked with UGT85C2, UGT74G1, and UGT76G1; native allele is suboptimal because R2-side activity is limiting
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Ambiguous donor specificity leads to shunt products containing rhamnose/xylose; incomplete relief of low R2-side flux even when expressed strongly
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves; substrate feeding with steviol or SG intermediates; yeast culture with supplied steviol
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Compared with rice OsUGT91C1, substrate positioning in UGT91D2 appears less favorable for R2-side glucosylation; catalytic dyad H38/D133 and PSPG-box residues are discussed structurally
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter in N. benthamiana; galactose-inducible pESC vectors in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics (DIA LC-MS/MS), ProteomeXchange: PXD056227; jPOST: JPST003380

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late steviol glycoside glycosylation; improved β-1,2 glucosylation especially on R2 side
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Engineered UGT91D2 variant carrying V155T that transfers glucose from UDP-glucose more efficiently to SG acceptors, especially for R2-side β-1,2 glucosylation, thereby enhancing conversion of Stv to Reb E and Reb A to Reb D and downstream Reb M formation while suppressing non-glucose donor side reactions
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Stv; Reb A; Rub; S13G; UDP-Glc
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Reb E; Reb D; Reb M; Sb; Stv
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target products and boosts downstream accumulation of Reb M from Reb D/Reb A pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Noncaloric natural sweeteners with superior sweetness and organoleptic properties
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves; heterologously expressed in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Engineered family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase; PSPG box-containing plant secondary product glycosyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Engineered improvement of the bottleneck step; strongly enhances the key R2-side control point
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Greatly increases Reb E, Reb D, and Reb M accumulation and reduces shunt products derived from rhamnose/xylose transfer
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Structure-guided amino acid substitution; yeast production assay from steviol; transient expression and substrate feeding in N. benthamiana; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; proteomics confirming comparable protein accumulation to WT
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. V155T markedly improved UGT91D2 catalytic performance, especially on the R2 side. 2. In yeast, Reb E, Reb D, and Reb M increased about 2.0- to 2.5-fold versus WT UGT91D2. 3. In N. benthamiana, the variant gave 3.4-fold more Reb E, 11.4-fold more Reb D, and 8.9- to 12.3-fold increases of Reb E/Reb D/Reb M in pathway reconstitution assays. 4. The variant also suppressed side products such as Dul A, Reb C, Reb F, Reb J, and Reb N, consistent with improved UDP-Glc specificity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value allele for precise genome editing or transgenic engineering of stevia to increase premium sweeteners Reb D and Reb M; suitable for stacking with UGT85C2, UGT74G1, and UGT76G1 in plants or microbial production systems; prime editing/base editing of the native UGT91D2 locus is explicitly proposed
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Although strongly improved, R2-side glucosylation remains limiting relative to precursor abundance; possible altered product spectrum due to reduced minor SG analog formation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves; substrate feeding with steviol or SG intermediates; yeast culture with supplied steviol
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Structural modeling suggests T155 forms a hydrogen bond with the C5''-CH2OH of UDP-Glc, shifting the donor sugar closer to catalytic H38 and improving donor specificity; suppression of UDP-Rh/UDP-Xy side reactions is a major mechanistic outcome
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter in N. benthamiana; galactose-inducible pESC vectors in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics (DIA LC-MS/MS), ProteomeXchange: PXD056227; jPOST: JPST003380

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Core early glycosylation steps from steviol aglycone
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Participates in conversion of steviol to early steviol glycoside intermediates in reconstructed pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for downstream Reb D and Reb M synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Coexpressed in yeast and N. benthamiana leaves for pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required pathway component enabling production of SG precursors from steviol in reconstituted systems
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coexpression in yeast and N. benthamiana with LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT85C2 was used as one of the four core stevia UGTs to reconstruct SG biosynthesis from steviol. 2. Together with UGT74G1 and UGT76G1, it supported formation of upstream intermediates required for downstream UGT91D2-dependent products. 3. Its individual catalytic contribution was not the main focus of this study.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as part of multigene stacking for SG pathway reconstruction in plants or microbes
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in N. benthamiana; yeast culture with steviol feeding
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is a primary experimental pathway component in the Results section, although not mechanistically dissected here
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S in N. benthamiana; galactose-inducible pESC in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics dataset includes transiently expressed UGT proteins: PXD056227; JPST003380

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Participates in conversion of steviol to steviol glycoside intermediates in reconstructed pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for downstream Reb D and Reb M synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Coexpressed in yeast and N. benthamiana leaves for pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required pathway component for SG intermediate formation in the reconstituted pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coexpression in yeast and N. benthamiana with LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT74G1 was part of the 3UGT module needed to produce SG precursors from steviol. 2. With UGT85C2 and UGT76G1 it enabled accumulation of pathway products that could be further glucosylated by UGT91D2. 3. Its individual function was not dissected in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful component for pathway stacking in SG bioengineering
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in N. benthamiana; yeast culture with steviol feeding
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as an experimentally coexpressed core pathway gene but not the principal mechanistic target
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S in N. benthamiana; galactose-inducible pESC in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics dataset includes transiently expressed UGT proteins: PXD056227; JPST003380

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late glucosylation/branching; contributes to highly glucosylated SG formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Participates in SG glycosylation steps in reconstructed pathway and is suggested to mediate β-1,3 glycosylation leading to Reb I from Reb A
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor and branch products relevant to Reb D/Reb M pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Coexpressed in yeast and N. benthamiana leaves for pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Described in discussion as critical for highly glucosylated and branched SG production, but not directly tested as bottleneck here
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports formation of highly glucosylated/branched SGs in the reconstructed pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coexpression in yeast and N. benthamiana with LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT76G1 was an essential coexpressed pathway gene in SG reconstruction from steviol. 2. The paper notes Reb I likely arises from Reb A through β-1,3 glycosylation mediated by UGT76G1 rather than UGT91D2. 3. It functions together with other UGTs to enable accumulation of downstream sweeteners.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important stacking gene for producing complex and highly glucosylated SGs in plants or microbes
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Ambiguous substrate specificity is noted in discussion for SG engineering context
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in N. benthamiana; yeast culture with steviol feeding
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although not the main engineering target in this paper, it is experimentally central to pathway reconstitution and minor product interpretation
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S in N. benthamiana; galactose-inducible pESC in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics dataset includes transiently expressed UGT proteins: PXD056227; JPST003380
